description: In an apartment kitchen, Chandler sits on a counter unit on the left. Phoebe and Joey sit at a round table, and Monica stands near the refrigerator holding two green bottles. The room is well-lit. Chandler points his fork toward Joey and says, "You."
Chandler lowers his fork and says, "Wait a minute."
Chandler adds, "Wait. You're telling me this actress person is the only woman you ever wanted who didn't want you back?" Monica sets the bottles on the table. Joey replies, "Yeah."
Joey nods slightly while holding a piece of food.
Chandler leans forward and says, "Oh, my God. Is this what it's like to be you?" Audience laughter is audible.
Monica sits down next to Phoebe and looks at Joey.
Monica shakes a grated cheese dispenser over her plate and says, "Well, you're really crazy about her."
Joey gestures with his hands, replying, "Oh, you have no idea. And and when we're on stage, I get to kiss her and touch"
Joey mimics gripping his chest and says, "-her but then she goes home with the director and it's like somebody's ripping out my heart."
Phoebe smiles and says, "It's so great to see you feeling like"
Phoebe smiles widely. Ross walks through the door on the right.
Ross approaches Monica and says, "hey,"
Ross places his hands on Monica's shoulders and says, "Monica. Dad called this morning, and Aunt Sylvia passed."
Monica stands up quickly, drops a cloth on the table, and repeatedly shouts while pumping her fists. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stops, clenches her fists with a wide smile, and looks at Ross. Ross and Joey stare at her.
Ross, keeping his hands by his side, says, "We were all pretty shaken up about it."
Phoebe raises a hand and says, "Wait, am I missing something, though? Because I always thought death was supposed to be sad in a way."
Ross looks at Monica. Monica places a hand on her chest.
Ross says, "Well, Aunt Sylvia was, well, not a nice person." Monica keeps her hand on her chest.
Monica nods slightly and says, "She was a cruel, cranky old bitch." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica picks up a cloth from the table.
Monica pats Ross's arm and says, "And I'm sorry she died."
Monica looks at Ross.
Monica touches Ross's arm again and asks, "Did dad say I get the dollhouse?" Ross nods and replies, "You get the doll house."
Monica points to herself and says, "I get the doll house." Her eyes widen and she smiles.
Chandler smiles.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Wow. A house for dolls. That is so cool."
Phoebe lowers her hands.
Phoebe adds, "When I was a kid, I had a barrel."
Joey looks at Phoebe.
Joey asks, "Phoebe, you had a barrel for a dollhouse?" Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "No, just a barrel." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler and Ross stand near the table. Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica says, "You know what? You can play with my dollhouse." Phoebe's eyes widen as she asks, "Really?" Monica replies, "Really."
Monica gestures with her hands and says, "Anytime you want. When I was younger, all I wanted to do was to play with this doll house."
Monica raises her hands, adding, "But no, it was to be looked at but never played with."
Chandler, holding a piece of food, says, "My grandmother used to say that exact same thing to me." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks forward.
Chandler adds, "More of a bathtub type situation." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross stands holding a plate.
Ross says, "Monica, she did give you one chance. She told Monica she could play with the doll house if she lost 20 pounds."
Monica walks toward the table and says, "Yeah? Well, look who's"
Monica points upward with a bottle and says, "losing weight now. Aunt Sylvia." She smiles and looks up. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica takes a drink from the bottle. The screen goes black.
The screen remains black.
Outside a building, a yellow taxi drives past the entrance to Bloomingdale's. The setting changes to a well-lit office interior.

Rachel and Chandler walk into an office where Sophie sits at a desk. Rachel says, "Hey, Sophie." Sophie replies, "Hey, Rach." Chandler adds, "Hey." Rachel looks at Chandler and says, "Thanks for lunch, Chandler. You, you know, didn't have to walk me all the way back up here."
Chandler touches Rachel's back and says, "Oh, that's okay. No problem."
Rachel tucks her hair behind her ear. Chandler looks at a clothing rack.
Rachel turns to Chandler and says, "Honey, you do realize we don't keep the women's lingerie here in the office."
Chandler turns toward Rachel. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler nods and says, "Yes, I realize that." He gestures with his hand and adds, "Summer catalog, that's the stuff."
Rachel picks up a magazine and hands it to Chandler.
Joanna walks into the office, looks at Rachel, and says, "Rachel, I need the Versace invoice. Hello."
Joanna looks at Chandler and says, "You don't work for me."
Rachel turns to Joanna.
Rachel says, "Joanna, this is my friend Chandler Bing."
Joanna looks at Chandler and says, "Chandler Bing. That's a great name."
Chandler points a finger and says, "Nice. It's Gaelic for thy turkey's." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler finishes, "done."
Joanna smiles. Rachel sits down at a desk.
Chandler points to the door and says, "So I'm going to go. Nice meeting you." Joanna replies, "You too." Joanna waves slightly and says, "Bye, Chandler."
Chandler exits the office. Joanna looks at Rachel.
Joanna crosses her arms and says, "So, what's wrong with him?"
Rachel, looking at some papers, gestures with her hand and replies, "Oh, nothing. He's just goofy like that. I actually hardly notice it." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel smiles and looks at Joanna.
In an office, Rachel sits at her desk looking off-screen.
Joanna asks from off-screen, "No, no, I mean, is he married or involved with"
Rachel shakes her head, gestures with her hands, and replies, "no, no, he's not married or involved with"
Rachel concludes, "anyone." Joanna, standing near the desk, points a finger at Rachel.
Joanna lowers her finger slightly as Rachel looks at her.
Joanna says, "Rachel?" Rachel begins to stand up from her desk.
Rachel finishes standing and places one hand on her hip.
Rachel waves a hand dismissively and says, "Actually, you know what? Forget it."
Rachel points to herself and says, "Well, I'll ask him for you if you want me to."
Joanna replies, "Would you? Or is it just too sad and desperate? And you know, something that Sophie would"
Sophie, sitting on the floor behind a low bookshelf, lifts her head and finishes the sentence, "do?" then states, "I am here."
Joanna, keeping her gaze on Rachel, says, "I know that."

Rachel smiles slightly and presses her hands together. The setting changes to Monica's apartment living room. A large, multi-story dollhouse sits on the coffee table. Chandler, Joey, and Ross sit in the living area nearby.
Monica stands beside the dollhouse, leaning toward it. "Look at oh," she says.
Chandler leans forward on the couch, looking closely at the dollhouse.
Chandler says, "Wallpaper's a little faded."
Monica leans closer to the dollhouse and replies, "That's okay. Carpet's a little loose."
Chandler turns his head to look at Monica.
Chandler says, "Hardwood floors?"
Audience laughter is audible as the group continues looking at the dollhouse.
Phoebe enters the apartment carrying a large cardboard box. "Hey," she says.
Phoebe spots the dollhouse, gasps, and says, "Ooh!"
Phoebe walks toward the dollhouse and says, "Oh, Monica, it's so beautiful."
Monica smiles widely, claps her hands, and says, "I know."
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe sets the box down on the coffee table next to the dollhouse.
Phoebe claps her hands and says, "So I'm here, ready to play. Okay. I brought a bunch of stuff for the house."
Phoebe pulls a large white ceramic dog statue out of the box and says, "So check it out. Ha."
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe sets the dog statue next to the dollhouse, the statue reaching almost as high as the house itself.
Monica points at the statue and asks, "What's this?" Phoebe gestures toward it and says, "That's a dog. Every house should have a dog."
Monica replies, "Not one that can pee on the roof."
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe looks down at the dog.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Maybe it's so big because the house was built on radioactive waste."
Audience laughter is audible. Chandler reaches over and picks up a small piece of crumpled white tissue paper from the coffee table.
Chandler holds up the tissue paper and asks, "And is this in case the house sneezes?"
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe points at the tissue paper.
Phoebe says, "No, that's the ghost for the attic."
Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica says, "I don't want a ghost." Phoebe replies, "Well, nobody wants a ghost."
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe looks down at the tissue paper.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "But you've got one because the house is sitting on ancient Indian burial ground."
Audience laughter is audible. Ross, sitting on a chair, leans forward resting his arms on the chair's back.
Ross says, "Wait a minute. The house was built on radioactive waste and an ancient Indian burial ground?"
Ross continues, "That would never happen."
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe looks at Ross.
Phoebe gestures dismissively toward Ross and says, "Okay, obviously you don't know much about the US."
Phoebe finishes, "Government."
Audience laughter is audible. The apartment door opens and Rachel walks in.
Rachel says, "Hey."
Rachel puts her bag down on a nearby counter and takes off her coat.
Rachel walks toward the living area and says, "I need to talk to you."
Ross stands up from his chair and says, "Sure. What's up?"
Rachel stops, looks at Ross, and says, "Oh, sorry. I meant Chandler."
Audience laughter is audible as Ross looks down, his expression dropping.
Ross looks away and says, "I know."
Audience laughter continues as Ross puts his hands in his pockets.
Ross turns to walk away and says, "Well, if something comes up"
Audience laughter is audible as Ross walks into the background. Rachel walks over and leans on the back of the couch where Chandler is sitting.
Rachel says, "oh, I'm glad you guys have passed that little awkward phase."
Audience laughter is audible. Chandler looks up at Rachel.
Rachel says, "Okay. My boss, Joanna. When you left, she started asking questions about you."
Chandler smiles and says, "Like what? She"
Chandler shifts on the couch, lifting his arms slightly.
Chandler pulls his fists back in a repetitive, alternating motion, saying, "dug my action, did she? Checking out the Chan Chan Man,"
Audience laughter is audible. Rachel stares at Chandler without smiling, her hands clasped together.
Rachel says, "that was surreal."
Rachel unclasps her hands.
Rachel gestures slightly and says, "Okay, what do you think? You interested at all?"
Chandler shrugs slightly and replies, "Yeah, she seemed cool, attractive. I'll do it."
Rachel stands up, smiling, and touches Chandler's shoulder. "Oh, thank you, Chandler. This is so great. She's going to love me."
Ross steps into the frame holding a plastic dinosaur.
Ross moves the plastic dinosaur toward the dollhouse and says in a deepened voice, "Dinosaur attack."
Ross moves the dinosaur closer to the dollhouse.
Ross says, "Quick, everybody into the house."

The scene remains on Ross moving the dinosaur as audience laughter begins. In a living room, Monica stands beside a large dollhouse, facing Phoebe, who kneels by a box and a large ceramic dog. Chandler sits on the couch.
Monica points at Phoebe and says, "All right, Phoebe."
Monica continues pointing and says, "You know what? That's it. That's it."
Monica lowers her hands.
Monica gestures with both hands and says, "No dinosaurs, no ghosts, no giant dogs. Okay?"
Monica continues gesturing and says, "They're not the right size, they're not Victorian and they just don't go."
Phoebe picks up the large ceramic dog.
Phoebe places the ceramic dog into the cardboard box and says, "Okay, fine."
Phoebe picks up a small plastic dinosaur and stands up as audience laughter sounds.
Phoebe turns away from Monica, looks at the dinosaur, and says, "Come, dinosaur. We're not welcome in the house of no imagination."
Ross steps into the frame next to Phoebe amid continued audience laughter.
Ross looks at Phoebe and says, "Pheebs."
Ross points to the dollhouse area and says, "While we're hovering around the subject," over audience laughter.
Ross continues, "I just have to say that dinosaurs they don't go rawr," with a brief pause before the final phrase.
Audience laughter sounds as Phoebe turns to Ross.
Phoebe looks at Ross and says, "The little ones do."
Phoebe walks away. Ross watches her, then looks toward Monica as audience laughter plays. The setting changes to the exterior of a building.
A marquee reads "LUCILLE LORTEL THEATRE" as a bus drives past. Inside a dimly lit theater with rows of red seats, Joey stands holding a mug while Kate walks down the steps in the background.
Joey turns towards Kate and says, "Hey, Kate." Kate replies, "Morning."
Joey gestures with his free hand and says, "Listen, I went to that restaurant you were talking about last week," as a man in a long coat walks down the stairs and says to Kate, "hey, lovely."
The man and Kate walk away from Joey.

The man puts his arm around Kate and says, "Come, talk to me for a minute." Joey keeps his gaze on them as they walk away and says, "And I ate the food. Was good. I ate the fish." Audience laughter follows his line.
Lauren walks up to Joey.
Lauren smiles, points to herself, and says, "Hi, I'm Lauren. Kate's Understudy."
Joey turns to her and says, "Oh, hey."
Joey says, "Joey Tribbiani."
Lauren smiles widely and says, "I know. I'm a big fan of yours."
Joey's eyes widen and he says, "What?"
Lauren laughs and smiles at Joey as audience laughter sounds.
Lauren says, "I used to schedule my classes so I could watch Dr. Drake Ramoray on Days of our Lives."
Joey smiles and says, "Get out of here. Really?"
Lauren nods amid audience laughter.
Lauren says, "Absolutely. Oh, but then they went and dropped you down that elevator shaft."
Joey nods and says, "They gave me the shaft, all right."
Lauren laughs and claps her hands together as audience laughter sounds.
Lauren continues laughing and says, "You're so funny."
Joey smiles at her.
Joey gestures towards her and says, "Listen, what are you doing after rehearsal? Do you want to get a drink or something?"
Lauren looks down briefly.
Lauren looks back at Joey and says, "Well,".
Joey's smile fades as he looks over Lauren's shoulder. In the background, the man in the long coat and Kate kiss, prompting audience laughter.
Joey looks back at Lauren and says, "yeah, sure. A drink sounds great."
Lauren smiles and says, "Cool," over audience laughter.
Lauren turns to leave and says, "I'll see you then."
Joey says, "All right."
Lauren walks away. Joey watches her go, then looks towards the seating area where Kate and the man are standing.
The man in the long coat places his hands on his hips and says, "All right. It's time to act my talking props." Audience laughter follows the line.
Joey sits at a small table next to Kate, both looking towards the man. The scene transitions with a superimposed night view of the New York City skyline.
The setting returns to the apartment living room. Rachel sits on the couch, Monica stands by the dollhouse, and Ross sits at the kitchen table with a laptop.
Ross looks up from his laptop and says, "You know, if you want to make the boys bathroom realistic you're going to have to put some teeny, tiny porno magazines behind the toilet."
Monica's lips press into a thin smile while Rachel looks at Ross, accompanied by audience laughter.
Phoebe opens the apartment door, carrying a large cardboard box structure, and says, "Hi."
Phoebe walks into the apartment and says, "Look, everybody. Look at my new doll house."
Phoebe carries the cardboard dollhouse toward the kitchen table as audience laughter sounds.
Phoebe sets the dollhouse on the table as Monica, Rachel, and Ross gather around it.
Phoebe opens the front of her cardboard dollhouse and says, "Look."
The group leans in to look at the brightly colored, decorated dollhouse. Ross points at a section of it.
Phoebe points inside the dollhouse and says, "Oh, okay. It's a slide instead of stairs."
Ross and Rachel smile as they examine the dollhouse amid audience laughter.
Monica stands with her arms crossed and says, "Very interesting, Phoebe."
Monica looks at Phoebe while the others continue looking at the dollhouse.
Rachel points at the dollhouse and asks, "What's this?"
Phoebe smiles and says, "The licorice room. You can eat all the furniture,"
Rachel and Ross smile at the dollhouse as audience laughter plays.
Phoebe points inside again and says, "and when guests come over, they can stay on the Tootsie Rollaway bed."
Ross laughs and points at the dollhouse over audience laughter.
Ross continues smiling at the dollhouse and says, "This is the coolest house ever."
Monica looks on with her arms crossed.
Phoebe places a small smoking stick into the dollhouse and asks, "Hey, does anyone want to join me in the Aroma Room?" Ross replies, "I would," followed by audience laughter.
Monica unfolds her arms and steps away from the table.
Monica smiles and says, "Hey, guys."
Monica holds up a miniature piece of furniture near her dollhouse and asks, "Did you see my new china cabinet?"
Ross, Phoebe, and Rachel keep their gaze on Phoebe's cardboard dollhouse. Audience laughter sounds.
Monica looks at the group, her smile fading as they remain focused on the cardboard dollhouse.
Monica lowers the miniature cabinet and looks down. The video ends.
In an apartment, Chandler enters through the front door, wearing a dark suit jacket over a patterned shirt. An off-screen speaker says, "Hey, my father's house does that."
Chandler walks toward the kitchen area. Rachel approaches him. Audience laughter sounds.
Rachel claps her hands together near her chest and says, "Oh, okay. How did it go? Tell me everything."
Chandler stops walking and replies, "Well, movie was great. Dinner was great. And there's nothing like a cool, crisp New York" while raising one hand slightly during his response.
Chandler continues, "evening. Of course, I didn't get to enjoy any of that because Joanna's such a big, dull dud." Audience laughter sounds.
The scene changes to an office. Rachel stands facing Joanna.
Joanna smiles widely and says, "Chandler is fantastic."
Joanna maintains her smile as Rachel looks at her.
Rachel replies, "What?" Joanna grabs Rachel's hands and says, "Oh, God. We just clicked. You know how people just click? Like, he came by to pick me up and I opened the door and"
Joanna continues, "it was just, like, click." Audience laughter sounds.
Joanna maintains her smile while holding Rachel's hands.
Rachel asks, "Did he tell you?" Joanna replies, "Oh, and he's got such a good heart. Doesn't he have a good heart?" Rachel places a hand on her chest and says, "Oh, I know."
Joanna smiles and nods.
Joanna clasps her hands at her chest and says, "And he's so sweet."

Joanna turns to Sophie, seated at a desk nearby, and says, "Listen, he said he was going to call, so put him straight through." Sophie smiles. Joanna turns back to Rachel. Rachel says, "Isn't this great? Don't spoil it."
The scene changes to a theater stage, where Joey holds Kate's arms, standing close to her.
Joey looks at Kate and says, "Come on, baby, don't go."
Joey continues, "Please? What do you say?" Kate looks back at him. A cell phone begins to ring.
Joey and Kate turn their heads toward the audience seating area.
A man sits in the theater seating with a bag on his lap, holding a phone to his ear. He says, "Hello? Oh, it's you. Just one sec."
The man holds the phone away from his ear and gestures with his free hand toward the stage.
The man looks toward the stage and says, "I am going to take this call."
The man lowers the phone slightly.
The man says, "When I continue, I hope that there will appear on stage this magical thing that, in the theater, we call" while gesturing with his free hand.
The man remains seated and looks toward the stage.
The man leans forward and says, "committing to the moment," pausing before "committing." He then stands up, holding the phone.
Joey crosses his arms. The man walks up the theater aisle, holding the phone to his ear. Joey and Kate watch him leave.
Joey gestures toward the aisle and says, "The guy's like a cartoon."
Kate turns to Joey.
Kate asks, "What do you see in him anyway?" Joey shrugs.
Kate continues, "He happens to be brilliant. Which is more than I can say for that sweater you're dating." Joey uncrosses his arms.
Joey walks past Kate.
Joey stops, turns back to Kate, and gestures with one hand as he says, "Hey, I'm not interested in her sweater. It's what's underneath her sweater that counts." Audience laughter sounds.
Kate briefly looks away, then turns her gaze back to Joey.
Joey crosses his arms again and asks, "And besides, since when do you care who I'm going out with?" Kate replies, "I don't care."
Joey looks at Kate.
Kate asks, "Why, do you want me to care?" Joey replies, "Do you want me to want you to care?" Audience laughter sounds.
Joey stares at Kate.
Joey leans forward slightly and asks, "Do you?"
Kate looks back at him.
Kate asks, "What?"
Joey opens his mouth to speak but stops as footsteps approach.
The man walks back down the theater aisle, wearing a grey coat, and says, "Okay, I'm afraid to say this but let's pick it up where we left off."
The man sets his bag down on a seat and sits. Joey steps toward Kate.
Joey grasps Kate's arms, and they stand close together, resuming their previous positions.
Joey looks at Kate and says, "Come on, baby, don't go."
Joey maintains his hold on Kate's arms.
Joey continues, "Please? What do you say?" Kate replies, "I've got no reason to stay."
Joey leans in and kisses Kate. They hold the kiss.
The man in the audience groans loudly and yells, "Stop," rubbing his face with both hands.
The man drops his hands from his face.
The man stands up quickly, extending his arms, and shouts, "it. You must stop." Joey and Kate step apart.
The man walks into the aisle, looking toward the stage.
The man points at the stage and says, "You are bad actors," his voice louder than his previous lines.
The man turns and begins walking up the aisle.
The man continues walking away and shouts, "This is a terrible play." Audience laughter sounds.
The man continues walking up the aisle.
The man walks through a set of double doors at the back of the theater and calls out, "I'll see you in the morning," before the doors close behind him.
Joey and Kate stand silently on stage, looking toward the back doors of the theater. Audience laughter continues.
The theater remains quiet as Joey and Kate hold their positions. The video fades to black.
